title: The Hill Neighborhood, St. Louis
content:
The Hill is an Italian American neighborhood south of Forest Park, ~12 minutes west of downtown. Bounded by Manchester Ave (north), Southwest/Columbia Ave (south), Kingshighway (east), Hampton Ave (west).

HISTORY: Italian immigrants arrived late 19th century to work clay mines. Mining camp evolved into self-sufficient community. Saint Ambrose parish founded 1903. Childhood home of Yogi Berra and Joe Garagiola. Brightly colored banners and Italian flag fire hydrants throughout.

DINING:
Historic: Volpi Foods (since 1902), Gioia's Deli (since 1918), Amighetti's (century-old), DiGregorio's (1971), John Viviano & Sons (1950), Urzi's Market (1926)
Restaurants: Anthonino's Taverna (famous toasted ravioli), Charlie Gitto's (one of America's most romantic restaurants), Collina Eatery, Milo's Bocce Garden (1902 Anheuser-Busch tavern), Guido's on The Hill (STL-style pizza + Spanish tapas)
More: Carnivore steakhouse, 2nd Shift Brewing, Rock Star Tacos, Sado sushi, Chris' Pancake, Chocolate Chocolate Chocolate (factory tours)

EVENTS: Italian Heritage Parade and Festa, Christmas on The Hill, Giro Della Montagna (Labor Day cycling since 1986)

LANDMARKS: Piazza Imo (fountains, prayer gardens, open 6AM-10PM), Berra Park

REAL ESTATE:
- Historic: early 20th century brick bungalows/Craftsman, $150,000-$400,000
- New: La Collina by McBride Homes from $500,000s
- Walkable to restaurants and shopping

EDUCATION: St. Ambrose Catholic School (PK-8, A- rating, 110+ years), Gateway STEM High School (magnet, lottery admission)
